questions.text: When you play video games — what actually draws you in? Are you more into story-driven single-player games where you can sink into a world, competitive multiplayer stuff where you can beat other people, relaxing indie games, strategy games where you're solving problems, or something else? And what's your tolerance for frustration — do you like a challenge that makes you work through it, or do you prefer games that are more about chill enjoyment than difficulty?

When you think about sustainability in your actual day-to-day life — beyond the philosophical stuff — what specific things do you actually do? Like, do you avoid certain products, offset carbon, bring your own bags/bottles, choose overland travel over flying when possible, buy secondhand, or anything like that? Is there a particular part of environmental impact that you prioritize, or is it more of a general awareness thing?

What are you reading right now — or what have you read recently that actually stuck with you? I'm curious about the specific books, authors, or topics that show up on your shelves. Do you tend to read fiction for escapism and non-fiction for learning, or do you blend both? Any favorite authors or genres you keep coming back to?

Do you have any photography interest beyond phone snapshots? Like, do you ever think about composition, lighting, or framing when you're taking a photo? Do you have a proper camera, or is that something you've wanted to explore? And when you take photos — do you edit them, share them on social media, or do they just live on your phone?

How do you think about your career trajectory and professional growth? Are you actively working toward something specific — like a certain role, skill set, reputation, or type of work — or are you more in a 'follow interesting opportunities' mode? Do you have any mentorship, coaching, or deliberate skill-building outside of what's required for your current job?
